001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.utils;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.lang.reflect.*;
021import java.util.*;
022import java.util.function.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
027
028/**
029 * Terse alias facade for the domain utility classes.
030 *
031 * <p>A single wildcard static import
032 * ({@code import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;}) exposes the entire
033 * short-name vocabulary. Almost every method here is a one-line delegation to a canonical,
034 * fully-descriptive method on a domain class — behavior is identical to calling the canonical
035 * method directly — and each such alias documents its target via an {@code @see} tag. Canonical
036 * implementations live in {@link ObjectUtils}, {@link StringUtils}, {@link CollectionUtils},
037 * {@link ClassUtils}, {@link ThrowableUtils}, {@link IoUtils}, {@link FileUtils},
038 * {@link AssertionUtils}, {@link PredicateUtils}, {@link SystemUtils}, {@link DateUtils}, and
039 * {@link ReflectionUtils}.
040 *
041 * <p><b>Documented exception:</b> the terse exception factories ({@code rex}/{@code brex}/
042 * {@code iaex}/{@code isex}/{@code uoex}/{@code uoroex}/{@code ioex}/{@code exex}) are
043 * self-contained — they construct the exception directly rather than delegating to a domain
044 * class — so that the domain {@code *Utils} classes can stay {@code Shorts}-free (they use the
045 * package-private {@code Exceptions} helper instead). These are the only methods in this class
046 * with no {@code @see} tag.
047 *
048 * <p><b>Disjointness invariant:</b> no {@code Shorts} alias name is identical to the canonical
049 * (full) method name of any method in any domain class. This lets a developer wildcard-import
050 * {@code Shorts.*} alongside any single domain {@code *Utils.*} with zero method-name
051 * collisions.
052 *
053 * <p>This class is the replacement for the former {@code Utils} grab-bag (now deleted): callers
054 * replace {@code import static ...utils.Utils.*} with {@code import static ...utils.Shorts.*}.
055 */
056@SuppressWarnings({
057   "java:S1118" // Utility facade with static methods only.
058})
059public class Shorts {
060
061   /** Constructor — this class is meant to be subclassed. */
062   protected Shorts() {}
063
064   // ---- ObjectUtils ----
065
066   /**
067    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified object is not null.
068    *
069    * @param o The object to check.
070    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the object is not null.
071    * @see ObjectUtils#isNotNull(Object)
072    */
073   public static boolean nn(Object o) { return ObjectUtils.isNotNull(o); }
074
075   /**
076    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified value is null.
077    *
078    * @param <T> The value type.
079    * @param v The value to check.
080    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the value is null.
081    * @see ObjectUtils#isNull(Object)
082    */
083   public static <T> boolean n(T v) { return ObjectUtils.isNull(v); }
084
085   /**
086    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified array is null or any of its elements are null.
087    *
088    * @param v The values to check.
089    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the array is null or contains a null element.
090    * @see ObjectUtils#isNull(Object...)
091    */
092   public static boolean n(Object...v) { return ObjectUtils.isNull(v); }
093
094   /**
095    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the two objects are equal (null-safe).
096    *
097    * @param <T> The object type.
098    * @param a The first object.
099    * @param b The second object.
100    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are equal.
101    * @see ObjectUtils#equal(Object,Object)
102    */
103   public static <T> boolean eq(T a, T b) { return ObjectUtils.equal(a, b); }
104
105   /**
106    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the two strings are equal, optionally ignoring case.
107    *
108    * @param ci If <jk>true</jk>, comparison is case-insensitive.
109    * @param a The first string.
110    * @param b The second string.
111    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the strings are equal.
112    * @see ObjectUtils#equal(boolean,String,String)
113    */
114   public static boolean eq(boolean ci, String a, String b) { return ObjectUtils.equal(ci, a, b); }
115
116   /**
117    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the two objects are equal using the specified equality test.
118    *
119    * @param <T> The first object type.
120    * @param <U> The second object type.
121    * @param a The first object.
122    * @param b The second object.
123    * @param t The equality predicate.
124    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are equal per the predicate.
125    * @see ObjectUtils#equal(Object,Object,BiPredicate)
126    */
127   public static <T,U> boolean eq(T a, U b, BiPredicate<T,U> t) { return ObjectUtils.equal(a, b, t); }
128
129   /**
130    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the first object equals any of the remaining objects.
131    *
132    * @param <T> The object type.
133    * @param a The object to look for.
134    * @param b The candidate values.
135    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a match is found.
136    * @see ObjectUtils#equalsAny(Object,Object...)
137    */
138   @SafeVarargs
139   public static <T> boolean eqa(T a, T...b) { return ObjectUtils.equalsAny(a, b); }
140
141   /**
142    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the two objects are equal ignoring case (via their string forms).
143    *
144    * @param a The first object.
145    * @param b The second object.
146    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are equal ignoring case.
147    * @see ObjectUtils#equalIgnoreCase(Object,Object)
148    */
149   public static boolean eqic(Object a, Object b) { return ObjectUtils.equalIgnoreCase(a, b); }
150
151   /**
152    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the two objects are not equal (null-safe).
153    *
154    * @param <T> The object type.
155    * @param a The first object.
156    * @param b The second object.
157    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are not equal.
158    * @see ObjectUtils#notEqual(Object,Object)
159    */
160   public static <T> boolean neq(T a, T b) { return ObjectUtils.notEqual(a, b); }
161
162   /**
163    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the two objects are not equal using the specified equality test.
164    *
165    * @param <T> The first object type.
166    * @param <U> The second object type.
167    * @param a The first object.
168    * @param b The second object.
169    * @param t The equality predicate.
170    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are not equal per the predicate.
171    * @see ObjectUtils#notEqual(Object,Object,BiPredicate)
172    */
173   public static <T,U> boolean neq(T a, U b, BiPredicate<T,U> t) { return ObjectUtils.notEqual(a, b, t); }
174
175   /**
176    * Compares two objects for order (null-safe).
177    *
178    * @param a The first object.
179    * @param b The second object.
180    * @return A negative, zero, or positive integer as {@code a} is less than, equal to, or greater than {@code b}.
181    * @see ObjectUtils#compare(Object,Object)
182    */
183   public static int cmp(Object a, Object b) { return ObjectUtils.compare(a, b); }
184
185   /**
186    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the first comparable is less than the second.
187    *
188    * @param <T> The comparable type.
189    * @param a The first value.
190    * @param b The second value.
191    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@code a < b}.
192    * @see ObjectUtils#lessThan(Comparable,Comparable)
193    */
194   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean lt(T a, T b) { return ObjectUtils.lessThan(a, b); }
195
196   /**
197    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the first comparable is less than or equal to the second.
198    *
199    * @param <T> The comparable type.
200    * @param a The first value.
201    * @param b The second value.
202    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@code a <= b}.
203    * @see ObjectUtils#lessThanOrEqual(Comparable,Comparable)
204    */
205   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean lte(T a, T b) { return ObjectUtils.lessThanOrEqual(a, b); }
206
207   /**
208    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the first comparable is greater than the second.
209    *
210    * @param <T> The comparable type.
211    * @param a The first value.
212    * @param b The second value.
213    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@code a > b}.
214    * @see ObjectUtils#greaterThan(Comparable,Comparable)
215    */
216   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean gt(T a, T b) { return ObjectUtils.greaterThan(a, b); }
217
218   /**
219    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the first comparable is greater than or equal to the second.
220    *
221    * @param <T> The comparable type.
222    * @param a The first value.
223    * @param b The second value.
224    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@code a >= b}.
225    * @see ObjectUtils#greaterThanOrEqual(Comparable,Comparable)
226    */
227   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean gte(T a, T b) { return ObjectUtils.greaterThanOrEqual(a, b); }
228
229   /**
230    * Returns the first non-null argument, or null if all are null.
231    *
232    * @param <T> The value type.
233    * @param t The candidate values in priority order.
234    * @return The first non-null value, or null.
235    * @see ObjectUtils#coalesce(Object...)
236    */
237   @SafeVarargs
238   public static <T> T or(T...t) { return ObjectUtils.coalesce(t); }
239
240   /**
241    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if any of the specified booleans are <jk>true</jk>.
242    *
243    * @param v The booleans to check.
244    * @return <jk>true</jk> if at least one value is <jk>true</jk>.
245    * @see ObjectUtils#anyTrue(boolean...)
246    */
247   @SafeVarargs
248   public static boolean or(boolean...v) { return ObjectUtils.anyTrue(v); }
249
250   /**
251    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if all of the specified booleans are <jk>true</jk>.
252    *
253    * @param v The booleans to check.
254    * @return <jk>true</jk> if every value is <jk>true</jk>.
255    * @see ObjectUtils#allTrue(boolean...)
256    */
257   @SafeVarargs
258   public static boolean and(boolean...v) { return ObjectUtils.allTrue(v); }
259
260   /**
261    * Returns the logical negation of the specified boolean.
262    *
263    * @param v The value to negate.
264    * @return {@code !v}.
265    * @see ObjectUtils#negate(boolean)
266    */
267   public static boolean not(boolean v) { return ObjectUtils.negate(v); }
268
269   /**
270    * Converts the specified object to a boolean.
271    *
272    * @param v The value to convert.
273    * @return The boolean value.
274    * @see ObjectUtils#toBoolean(Object)
275    */
276   public static boolean b(Object v) { return ObjectUtils.toBoolean(v); }
277
278   /**
279    * Converts the specified object to its string representation (null-safe).
280    *
281    * @param v The value to stringify.
282    * @return The string form, or null if the value is null.
283    * @see ObjectUtils#stringify(Object)
284    */
285   public static String s(Object v) { return ObjectUtils.stringify(v); }
286
287   /**
288    * Returns the system identity string of the specified object.
289    *
290    * @param o The object.
291    * @return The identity string (class name + identity hashcode).
292    * @see ObjectUtils#identity(Object)
293    */
294   public static String id(Object o) { return ObjectUtils.identity(o); }
295
296   /**
297    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified integer is within the inclusive range.
298    *
299    * @param n The value to test.
300    * @param lo The inclusive lower bound.
301    * @param hi The inclusive upper bound.
302    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@code lo <= n <= hi}.
303    * @see ObjectUtils#isBetween(int,int,int)
304    */
305   public static boolean btw(int n, int lo, int hi) { return ObjectUtils.isBetween(n, lo, hi); }
306
307   /**
308    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified number is non-null and not equal to -1.
309    *
310    * @param <T> The number type.
311    * @param v The value to test.
312    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the value is not -1.
313    * @see ObjectUtils#isNotMinusOne(Number)
314    */
315   public static <T extends Number> boolean nm1(T v) { return ObjectUtils.isNotMinusOne(v); }
316
317   /**
318    * Computes a hash code over the specified values.
319    *
320    * @param v The values to hash.
321    * @return The combined hash code.
322    * @see ObjectUtils#hash(Object...)
323    */
324   public static int h(Object...v) { return ObjectUtils.hash(v); }
325
326   /**
327    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified object is null or empty (string/collection/map/array).
328    *
329    * @param o The object to check.
330    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the object is null or empty.
331    * @see ObjectUtils#isEmpty(Object)
332    */
333   public static boolean ie(Object o) { return ObjectUtils.isEmpty(o); }
334
335   /**
336    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified object is not null and not empty.
337    *
338    * @param o The object to check.
339    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the object is non-empty.
340    * @see ObjectUtils#isNotEmpty(Object)
341    */
342   public static boolean ine(Object o) { return ObjectUtils.isNotEmpty(o); }
343
344   /**
345    * Wraps the specified value in an {@link Optional}.
346    *
347    * @param <T> The value type.
348    * @param t The value (may be null).
349    * @return An optional containing the value, or empty if null.
350    * @see ObjectUtils#optional(Object)
351    */
352   public static <T> Optional<T> o(T t) { return ObjectUtils.optional(t); }
353
354   /**
355    * Returns the element at the specified index of a list as an {@link Optional}.
356    *
357    * @param <T> The element type.
358    * @param l The list.
359    * @param i The index.
360    * @return An optional containing the element, or empty if out of bounds.
361    * @see CollectionUtils#optionalAt(List,int)
362    */
363   public static <T> Optional<T> o(List<T> l, int i) { return CollectionUtils.optionalAt(l, i); }
364
365   /**
366    * Returns an empty {@link Optional}.
367    *
368    * @param <T> The value type.
369    * @return An empty optional.
370    * @see ObjectUtils#emptyOptional()
371    */
372   public static <T> Optional<T> oe() { return ObjectUtils.emptyOptional(); }
373
374   /**
375    * Returns the value of the optional, or the specified default if empty.
376    *
377    * @param <T> The value type.
378    * @param o The optional.
379    * @param d The default value.
380    * @return The optional's value, or {@code d} if empty.
381    * @see ObjectUtils#orElse(Optional,Object)
382    */
383   public static <T> T oo(Optional<T> o, T d) { return ObjectUtils.orElse(o, d); }
384
385   /**
386    * Returns the specified value, throwing {@link NullPointerException} if it is null.
387    *
388    * @param <T> The value type.
389    * @param v The value.
390    * @return The non-null value.
391    * @see ObjectUtils#requireNonNull(Object)
392    */
393   public static <T> T rnn(T v) { return ObjectUtils.requireNonNull(v); }
394
395   /**
396    * Returns the size of the specified object (string/collection/map/array).
397    *
398    * @param o The object.
399    * @return The size, or 0 if null.
400    * @see ObjectUtils#size(Object)
401    */
402   public static int sz(Object o) { return ObjectUtils.size(o); }
403
404   // ---- StringUtils ----
405
406   /**
407    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified character sequence is null or empty.
408    *
409    * @param s The sequence to check.
410    * @return <jk>true</jk> if null or zero-length.
411    * @see StringUtils#isEmpty(CharSequence)
412    */
413   public static boolean ie(CharSequence s) { return StringUtils.isEmpty(s); }
414
415   /**
416    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified character sequence is not null and not empty.
417    *
418    * @param s The sequence to check.
419    * @return <jk>true</jk> if non-empty.
420    * @see StringUtils#isNotEmpty(CharSequence)
421    */
422   public static boolean ine(CharSequence s) { return StringUtils.isNotEmpty(s); }
423
424   /**
425    * Formats a message using {@link java.text.MessageFormat}-style {0} placeholders.
426    *
427    * @param p The pattern.
428    * @param a The arguments.
429    * @return The formatted string.
430    * @see StringUtils#format(String,Object...)
431    */
432   public static String f(String p, Object...a) { return StringUtils.format(p, a); }
433
434   /**
435    * Returns a supplier that lazily formats a message (deferred until {@link Supplier#get()}).
436    *
437    * @param p The pattern.
438    * @param a The arguments.
439    * @return A supplier of the formatted string.
440    * @see StringUtils#format(String,Object...)
441    */
442   public static Supplier<String> fs(String p, Object...a) { return () -> StringUtils.format(p, a); }
443
444   /**
445    * Returns a human-readable string representation of the specified object.
446    *
447    * @param o The object.
448    * @return The readable representation.
449    * @see StringUtils#readable(Object)
450    */
451   public static String r(Object o) { return StringUtils.readable(o); }
452
453   /**
454    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified string is null, empty, or whitespace-only.
455    *
456    * @param s The string to check.
457    * @return <jk>true</jk> if blank.
458    * @see StringUtils#isBlank(CharSequence)
459    */
460   public static boolean ib(String s) { return StringUtils.isBlank(s); }
461
462   /**
463    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified string contains non-whitespace content.
464    *
465    * @param s The string to check.
466    * @return <jk>true</jk> if not blank.
467    * @see StringUtils#isNotBlank(CharSequence)
468    */
469   public static boolean inb(String s) { return StringUtils.isNotBlank(s); }
470
471   /**
472    * Converts the specified string to lower case (null-safe).
473    *
474    * @param s The string.
475    * @return The lower-cased string, or null if null.
476    * @see StringUtils#lowerCase(String)
477    */
478   public static String lc(String s) { return StringUtils.lowerCase(s); }
479
480   /**
481    * Converts the specified string to upper case (null-safe).
482    *
483    * @param s The string.
484    * @return The upper-cased string, or null if null.
485    * @see StringUtils#upperCase(String)
486    */
487   public static String uc(String s) { return StringUtils.upperCase(s); }
488
489   /**
490    * Trims leading/trailing whitespace from the specified string (null-safe).
491    *
492    * @param s The string.
493    * @return The trimmed string, or null if null.
494    * @see StringUtils#trim(String)
495    */
496   public static String tr(String s) { return StringUtils.trim(s); }
497
498   /**
499    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the string starts with the specified prefix (null-safe).
500    *
501    * @param s The string.
502    * @param p The prefix.
503    * @return <jk>true</jk> if it starts with the prefix.
504    * @see StringUtils#startsWith(String,String)
505    */
506   public static boolean sw(String s, String p) { return StringUtils.startsWith(s, p); }
507
508   /**
509    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the string ends with the specified suffix (null-safe).
510    *
511    * @param s The string.
512    * @param x The suffix.
513    * @return <jk>true</jk> if it ends with the suffix.
514    * @see StringUtils#endsWith(String,String)
515    */
516   public static boolean ew(String s, String x) { return StringUtils.endsWith(s, x); }
517
518   /**
519    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the string contains the specified substring (null-safe).
520    *
521    * @param s The string.
522    * @param x The substring.
523    * @return <jk>true</jk> if it contains the substring.
524    * @see StringUtils#contains(String,String)
525    */
526   public static boolean co(String s, String x) { return StringUtils.contains(s, x); }
527
528   /**
529    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the two strings are equal ignoring case (null-safe).
530    *
531    * @param a The first string.
532    * @param b The second string.
533    * @return <jk>true</jk> if equal ignoring case.
534    * @see StringUtils#equalsIgnoreCase(String,String)
535    */
536   public static boolean eqic(String a, String b) { return StringUtils.equalsIgnoreCase(a, b); }
537
538   /**
539    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the two strings are not equal ignoring case (null-safe).
540    *
541    * @param a The first string.
542    * @param b The second string.
543    * @return <jk>true</jk> if not equal ignoring case.
544    * @see StringUtils#notEqualsIgnoreCase(String,String)
545    */
546   public static boolean neqic(String a, String b) { return StringUtils.notEqualsIgnoreCase(a, b); }
547
548   /**
549    * Returns an empty string if the specified string is null, else the string.
550    *
551    * @param s The string.
552    * @return The string, or an empty string if null.
553    * @see StringUtils#emptyIfNull(String)
554    */
555   public static String ein(String s) { return StringUtils.emptyIfNull(s); }
556
557   /**
558    * Returns an empty string if the specified object is null, else its string form.
559    *
560    * @param o The object.
561    * @return The string form, or an empty string if null.
562    * @see StringUtils#emptyIfNull(Object)
563    */
564   public static String ein(Object o) { return StringUtils.emptyIfNull(o); }
565
566   /**
567    * Returns a blank (single-space) string if the specified string is null, else the string.
568    *
569    * @param s The string.
570    * @return The string, or a blank string if null.
571    * @see StringUtils#blankIfNull(String)
572    */
573   public static String bin(String s) { return StringUtils.blankIfNull(s); }
574
575   /**
576    * Returns null if the specified string is empty, else the string.
577    *
578    * @param s The string.
579    * @return The string, or null if empty.
580    * @see StringUtils#nullIfEmpty(String)
581    */
582   public static String nie(String s) { return StringUtils.nullIfEmpty(s); }
583
584   /**
585    * Creates a new {@link StringBuilder} initialized with the stringified form of the specified value.
586    *
587    * @param v The initial value. Can be <jk>null</jk> (renders as {@code "null"}).
588    * @return A new string builder.
589    * @see StringUtils#newStringBuilder(Object)
590    */
591   public static StringBuilder sb(Object v) { return StringUtils.newStringBuilder(v); }
592
593   /**
594    * Creates a new {@link StringBuilder} with the stringified form of each specified value appended in order.
595    *
596    * @param v The values to append in order (empty builder for no args).
597    * @return A new string builder.
598    * @see StringUtils#newStringBuilder(Object...)
599    */
600   public static StringBuilder sb(Object...v) { return StringUtils.newStringBuilder(v); }
601
602   /**
603    * Returns the fully-qualified class name of the specified object (null-safe).
604    *
605    * @param o The object.
606    * @return The class name, or null if the object is null.
607    * @see ClassUtils#className(Object)
608    */
609   public static String cn(Object o) { return ClassUtils.className(o); }
610
611   /**
612    * Returns the simple class name of the specified object (null-safe).
613    *
614    * @param o The object.
615    * @return The simple class name, or null if the object is null.
616    * @see ClassUtils#classNameSimple(Object)
617    */
618   public static String cns(Object o) { return ClassUtils.classNameSimple(o); }
619
620   /**
621    * Returns the qualified simple class name of the specified object (includes enclosing classes).
622    *
623    * @param o The object.
624    * @return The qualified simple class name.
625    * @see ClassUtils#classNameSimpleQualified(Object)
626    */
627   public static String cnsq(Object o) { return ClassUtils.classNameSimpleQualified(o); }
628
629   /**
630    * Returns the first non-empty string from the specified values.
631    *
632    * @param v The candidate strings.
633    * @return The first non-empty string, or null if all are empty.
634    * @see StringUtils#firstNonEmpty(String...)
635    */
636   @SafeVarargs
637   public static String fne(String...v) { return StringUtils.firstNonEmpty(v); }
638
639   /**
640    * Returns the first non-blank string from the specified values.
641    *
642    * @param v The candidate strings.
643    * @return The first non-blank string, or null if all are blank.
644    * @see StringUtils#firstNonBlank(String...)
645    */
646   @SafeVarargs
647   public static String fnb(String...v) { return StringUtils.firstNonBlank(v); }
648
649   /**
650    * Concatenates the specified strings.
651    *
652    * @param v The strings to join.
653    * @return The concatenated string.
654    * @see StringUtils#join(String...)
655    */
656   @SafeVarargs
657   public static String jn(String...v) { return StringUtils.join(v); }
658
659   // ---- CollectionUtils ----
660
661   /**
662    * Creates an array from the specified elements.
663    *
664    * @param <T> The element type.
665    * @param x The elements.
666    * @return A new array.
667    * @see CollectionUtils#array(Object...)
668    */
669   @SafeVarargs
670   public static <T> T[] a(T...x) { return CollectionUtils.array(x); }
671
672   /**
673    * Creates a modifiable {@link ArrayList} from the specified elements.
674    *
675    * @param <T> The element type.
676    * @param x The elements.
677    * @return A new modifiable list.
678    * @see CollectionUtils#list(Object...)
679    */
680   @SafeVarargs
681   public static <T> List<T> l(T...x) { return CollectionUtils.list(x); }
682
683   /**
684    * Creates an empty modifiable map.
685    *
686    * @param <K> The key type.
687    * @param <V> The value type.
688    * @return An empty modifiable map.
689    * @see CollectionUtils#map()
690    */
691   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m() { return CollectionUtils.map(); }
692
693   /**
694    * Creates a modifiable map with 1 entry.
695    *
696    * @param <K> The key type.
697    * @param <V> The value type.
698    * @param k1 The key.
699    * @param v1 The value.
700    * @return A modifiable map.
701    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object)
702    */
703   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1); }
704
705   /**
706    * Creates a modifiable map with 2 entries.
707    *
708    * @param <K> The key type.
709    * @param <V> The value type.
710    * @param k1 The first key.
711    * @param v1 The first value.
712    * @param k2 The second key.
713    * @param v2 The second value.
714    * @return A modifiable map.
715    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object)
716    */
717   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2); }
718
719   /**
720    * Creates a modifiable map with 3 entries.
721    *
722    * @param <K> The key type.
723    * @param <V> The value type.
724    * @param k1 The first key.
725    * @param v1 The first value.
726    * @param k2 The second key.
727    * @param v2 The second value.
728    * @param k3 The third key.
729    * @param v3 The third value.
730    * @return A modifiable map.
731    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)
732    */
733   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2, k3, v3); }
734
735   /**
736    * Creates a modifiable map with 4 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
737    *
738    * @param <K> The key type.
739    * @param <V> The value type.
740    * @param k1 Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2.
741    * @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4.
742    * @return A modifiable map.
743    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)
744    */
745   @SuppressWarnings({
746      "java:S107" // Fixed-arity terse map-factory overload; the many parameters are intentional alternating key/value pairs mirroring map entries.
747   })
748   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2, k3, v3, k4, v4); }
749
750   /**
751    * Creates a modifiable map with 5 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
752    *
753    * @param <K> The key type.
754    * @param <V> The value type.
755    * @param k1 Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3.
756    * @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4. @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5.
757    * @return A modifiable map.
758    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)
759    */
760   @SuppressWarnings({
761      "java:S107" // Fixed-arity terse map-factory overload; the many parameters are intentional alternating key/value pairs mirroring map entries.
762   })
763   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2, k3, v3, k4, v4, k5, v5); }
764
765   /**
766    * Creates a modifiable map with 6 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
767    *
768    * @param <K> The key type.
769    * @param <V> The value type.
770    * @param k1 Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3.
771    * @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4. @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5. @param k6 Key 6. @param v6 Value 6.
772    * @return A modifiable map.
773    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)
774    */
775   @SuppressWarnings({
776      "java:S107" // Fixed-arity terse map-factory overload; the many parameters are intentional alternating key/value pairs mirroring map entries.
777   })
778   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2, k3, v3, k4, v4, k5, v5, k6, v6); }
779
780   /**
781    * Creates a modifiable map with 7 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
782    *
783    * @param <K> The key type.
784    * @param <V> The value type.
785    * @param k1 Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4.
786    * @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5. @param k6 Key 6. @param v6 Value 6. @param k7 Key 7. @param v7 Value 7.
787    * @return A modifiable map.
788    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)
789    */
790   @SuppressWarnings({
791      "java:S107" // Fixed-arity terse map-factory overload; the many parameters are intentional alternating key/value pairs mirroring map entries.
792   })
793   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2, k3, v3, k4, v4, k5, v5, k6, v6, k7, v7); }
794
795   /**
796    * Creates a modifiable map with 8 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
797    *
798    * @param <K> The key type.
799    * @param <V> The value type.
800    * @param k1 Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4.
801    * @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5. @param k6 Key 6. @param v6 Value 6. @param k7 Key 7. @param v7 Value 7. @param k8 Key 8. @param v8 Value 8.
802    * @return A modifiable map.
803    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)
804    */
805   @SuppressWarnings({
806      "java:S107" // Fixed-arity terse map-factory overload; the many parameters are intentional alternating key/value pairs mirroring map entries.
807   })
808   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2, k3, v3, k4, v4, k5, v5, k6, v6, k7, v7, k8, v8); }
809
810   /**
811    * Creates a modifiable map with 9 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
812    *
813    * @param <K> The key type.
814    * @param <V> The value type.
815    * @param k1 Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4. @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5.
816    * @param k6 Key 6. @param v6 Value 6. @param k7 Key 7. @param v7 Value 7. @param k8 Key 8. @param v8 Value 8. @param k9 Key 9. @param v9 Value 9.
817    * @return A modifiable map.
818    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)
819    */
820   @SuppressWarnings({
821      "java:S107" // Fixed-arity terse map-factory overload; the many parameters are intentional alternating key/value pairs mirroring map entries.
822   })
823   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8, K k9, V v9) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2, k3, v3, k4, v4, k5, v5, k6, v6, k7, v7, k8, v8, k9, v9); }
824
825   /**
826    * Creates a modifiable map with 10 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
827    *
828    * @param <K> The key type.
829    * @param <V> The value type.
830    * @param k1 Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4. @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5.
831    * @param k6 Key 6. @param v6 Value 6. @param k7 Key 7. @param v7 Value 7. @param k8 Key 8. @param v8 Value 8. @param k9 Key 9. @param v9 Value 9. @param k10 Key 10. @param v10 Value 10.
832    * @return A modifiable map.
833    * @see CollectionUtils#map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)
834    */
835   @SuppressWarnings({
836      "java:S107" // Fixed-arity terse map-factory overload; the many parameters are intentional alternating key/value pairs mirroring map entries.
837   })
838   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8, K k9, V v9, K k10, V v10) { return CollectionUtils.map(k1, v1, k2, v2, k3, v3, k4, v4, k5, v5, k6, v6, k7, v7, k8, v8, k9, v9, k10, v10); }
839
840   /**
841    * Returns the element at the specified index of a list (null-safe).
842    *
843    * @param <E> The element type.
844    * @param l The list.
845    * @param i The index.
846    * @return The element, or null if out of bounds.
847    * @see CollectionUtils#elementAt(List,int)
848    */
849   public static <E> E at(List<E> l, int i) { return CollectionUtils.elementAt(l, i); }
850
851   /**
852    * Creates an empty (zero-length) array of the specified component type.
853    *
854    * @param <T> The component type.
855    * @param t The component class.
856    * @return An empty typed array.
857    * @see CollectionUtils#nullArray(Class)
858    */
859   public static <T> T[] na(Class<T> t) { return CollectionUtils.nullArray(t); }
860
861   /**
862    * Returns null if the specified map is empty, else the map.
863    *
864    * @param <K> The key type.
865    * @param <V> The value type.
866    * @param v The map.
867    * @return The map, or null if empty.
868    * @see CollectionUtils#nullIfEmpty(Map)
869    */
870   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> nie(Map<K,V> v) { return CollectionUtils.nullIfEmpty(v); }
871
872   /**
873    * Returns null if the specified list is empty, else the list.
874    *
875    * @param <E> The element type.
876    * @param v The list.
877    * @return The list, or null if empty.
878    * @see CollectionUtils#nullIfEmpty(List)
879    */
880   public static <E> List<E> nie(List<E> v) { return CollectionUtils.nullIfEmpty(v); }
881
882   /**
883    * Returns null if the specified set is empty, else the set.
884    *
885    * @param <E> The element type.
886    * @param v The set.
887    * @return The set, or null if empty.
888    * @see CollectionUtils#nullIfEmpty(Set)
889    */
890   public static <E> Set<E> nie(Set<E> v) { return CollectionUtils.nullIfEmpty(v); }
891
892   /**
893    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified collection is null or empty.
894    *
895    * @param c The collection.
896    * @return <jk>true</jk> if null or empty.
897    * @see CollectionUtils#isEmpty(Collection)
898    */
899   public static boolean ie(Collection<?> c) { return CollectionUtils.isEmpty(c); }
900
901   /**
902    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified map is null or empty.
903    *
904    * @param m The map.
905    * @return <jk>true</jk> if null or empty.
906    * @see CollectionUtils#isEmpty(Map)
907    */
908   public static boolean ie(Map<?,?> m) { return CollectionUtils.isEmpty(m); }
909
910   /**
911    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified collection is not null and not empty.
912    *
913    * @param c The collection.
914    * @return <jk>true</jk> if non-empty.
915    * @see CollectionUtils#isNotEmpty(Collection)
916    */
917   public static boolean ine(Collection<?> c) { return CollectionUtils.isNotEmpty(c); }
918
919   /**
920    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified map is not null and not empty.
921    *
922    * @param m The map.
923    * @return <jk>true</jk> if non-empty.
924    * @see CollectionUtils#isNotEmpty(Map)
925    */
926   public static boolean ine(Map<?,?> m) { return CollectionUtils.isNotEmpty(m); }
927
928   /**
929    * Creates a 2-dimensional array from the specified rows.
930    *
931    * @param <E> The element type.
932    * @param v The rows.
933    * @return A 2-dimensional array.
934    * @see CollectionUtils#array2d(Object[][])
935    */
936   @SafeVarargs
937   public static <E> E[][] a2(E[]...v) { return CollectionUtils.array2d(v); }
938
939   /**
940    * Creates an {@code Object[]} from the specified elements.
941    *
942    * @param v The elements.
943    * @return A new object array.
944    * @see CollectionUtils#objectArray(Object...)
945    */
946   public static Object[] ao(Object...v) { return CollectionUtils.objectArray(v); }
947
948   /**
949    * Creates a mutable {@link LinkedList} from the specified elements.
950    *
951    * @param <T> The element type.
952    * @param v The elements.
953    * @return A new linked list.
954    * @see CollectionUtils#linkedList(Object...)
955    */
956   @SafeVarargs
957   public static <T> List<T> ll(T...v) { return CollectionUtils.linkedList(v); }
958
959   /**
960    * Creates a mutable {@link HashSet} from the specified elements.
961    *
962    * @param <T> The element type.
963    * @param v The elements.
964    * @return A new hash set.
965    * @see CollectionUtils#hashSet(Object...)
966    */
967   @SafeVarargs
968   public static <T> Set<T> hs(T...v) { return CollectionUtils.hashSet(v); }
969
970   /**
971    * Creates a modifiable, insertion-ordered {@link LinkedHashSet} from the specified elements.
972    *
973    * @param <T> The element type.
974    * @param v The elements.
975    * @return A new modifiable set.
976    * @see CollectionUtils#set(Object...)
977    */
978   @SafeVarargs
979   public static <T> Set<T> st(T...v) { return CollectionUtils.set(v); }
980
981   /**
982    * Creates a sorted {@link TreeSet} from the specified elements (null values filtered out).
983    *
984    * @param <E> The element type.
985    * @param v The elements.
986    * @return A new sorted set.
987    * @see CollectionUtils#sortedSet(Object...)
988    */
989   @SafeVarargs
990   public static <E> SortedSet<E> ss(E...v) { return CollectionUtils.sortedSet(v); }
991
992   /**
993    * Returns an unmodifiable view of the specified list.
994    *
995    * @param <T> The element type.
996    * @param v The list.
997    * @return An unmodifiable list.
998    * @see CollectionUtils#unmodifiable(List)
999    */
1000   public static <T> List<T> u(List<? extends T> v) { return CollectionUtils.unmodifiable(v); }
1001
1002   /**
1003    * Returns an unmodifiable view of the specified map.
1004    *
1005    * @param <K> The key type.
1006    * @param <V> The value type.
1007    * @param v The map.
1008    * @return An unmodifiable map.
1009    * @see CollectionUtils#unmodifiable(Map)
1010    */
1011   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> u(Map<? extends K,? extends V> v) { return CollectionUtils.unmodifiable(v); }
1012
1013   /**
1014    * Returns an unmodifiable view of the specified set.
1015    *
1016    * @param <T> The element type.
1017    * @param v The set.
1018    * @return An unmodifiable set.
1019    * @see CollectionUtils#unmodifiable(Set)
1020    */
1021   public static <T> Set<T> u(Set<? extends T> v) { return CollectionUtils.unmodifiable(v); }
1022
1023   /**
1024    * Returns an unmodifiable view of the specified sorted set.
1025    *
1026    * @param <T> The element type.
1027    * @param v The sorted set.
1028    * @return An unmodifiable sorted set.
1029    * @see CollectionUtils#unmodifiable(SortedSet)
1030    */
1031   public static <T> SortedSet<T> u(SortedSet<T> v) { return CollectionUtils.unmodifiable(v); }
1032
1033   /**
1034    * Returns the length/size of the specified object (array/collection/map/string).
1035    *
1036    * @param v The object.
1037    * @return The length, or 0 if null.
1038    * @see CollectionUtils#length(Object)
1039    */
1040   public static int len(Object v) { return CollectionUtils.length(v); }
1041
1042   /**
1043    * Returns a mutable copy of the specified list.
1044    *
1045    * @param <E> The element type.
1046    * @param v The list to copy.
1047    * @return A new list containing the same elements.
1048    * @see CollectionUtils#copyOf(List)
1049    */
1050   public static <E> List<E> cp(List<E> v) { return CollectionUtils.copyOf(v); }
1051
1052   /**
1053    * Adds the specified elements to the given list and returns it.
1054    *
1055    * @param <E> The element type.
1056    * @param v The list to add to.
1057    * @param e The elements to add.
1058    * @return The same list.
1059    * @see CollectionUtils#addAll(List,Object...)
1060    */
1061   @SafeVarargs
1062   public static <E> List<E> aa(List<E> v, E...e) { return CollectionUtils.addAll(v, e); }
1063
1064   /**
1065    * Creates an {@code int[]} from the specified values.
1066    *
1067    * @param v The values.
1068    * @return A new int array.
1069    * @see CollectionUtils#intArray(int...)
1070    */
1071   public static int[] ints(int...v) { return CollectionUtils.intArray(v); }
1072
1073   /**
1074    * Creates a {@code long[]} from the specified values.
1075    *
1076    * @param v The values.
1077    * @return A new long array.
1078    * @see CollectionUtils#longArray(long...)
1079    */
1080   public static long[] longs(long...v) { return CollectionUtils.longArray(v); }
1081
1082   /**
1083    * Creates a {@code byte[]} from the specified int values.
1084    *
1085    * @param v The values.
1086    * @return A new byte array.
1087    * @see CollectionUtils#byteArray(int...)
1088    */
1089   public static byte[] bytes(int...v) { return CollectionUtils.byteArray(v); }
1090
1091   /**
1092    * Creates a {@code char[]} from the specified values.
1093    *
1094    * @param v The values.
1095    * @return A new char array.
1096    * @see CollectionUtils#charArray(char...)
1097    */
1098   public static char[] chars(char...v) { return CollectionUtils.charArray(v); }
1099
1100   /**
1101    * Creates a {@code float[]} from the specified values.
1102    *
1103    * @param v The values.
1104    * @return A new float array.
1105    * @see CollectionUtils#floatArray(float...)
1106    */
1107   public static float[] floats(float...v) { return CollectionUtils.floatArray(v); }
1108
1109   /**
1110    * Creates a {@code double[]} from the specified values.
1111    *
1112    * @param v The values.
1113    * @return A new double array.
1114    * @see CollectionUtils#doubleArray(double...)
1115    */
1116   public static double[] doubles(double...v) { return CollectionUtils.doubleArray(v); }
1117
1118   /**
1119    * Creates a {@code boolean[]} from the specified values.
1120    *
1121    * @param v The values.
1122    * @return A new boolean array.
1123    * @see CollectionUtils#booleanArray(boolean...)
1124    */
1125   public static boolean[] booleans(boolean...v) { return CollectionUtils.booleanArray(v); }
1126
1127   /**
1128    * Creates a {@code short[]} from the specified int values.
1129    *
1130    * @param v The values.
1131    * @return A new short array.
1132    * @see CollectionUtils#shortArray(int...)
1133    */
1134   public static short[] shorts(int...v) { return CollectionUtils.shortArray(v); }
1135
1136   /**
1137    * Creates a new {@link Lists} builder for the specified element type.
1138    *
1139    * @param <E> The element type.
1140    * @param type The element type.
1141    * @return A new list builder.
1142    * @see CollectionUtils#listBuilder(Class)
1143    */
1144   public static <E> Lists<E> lb(Class<E> type) { return CollectionUtils.listBuilder(type); }
1145
1146   /**
1147    * Creates a new {@link Maps} builder.
1148    *
1149    * @param <K> The key type.
1150    * @param <V> The value type.
1151    * @return A new map builder.
1152    * @see CollectionUtils#mapBuilder()
1153    */
1154   public static <K,V> Maps<K,V> mb() { return CollectionUtils.mapBuilder(); }
1155
1156   /**
1157    * Creates a new {@link Maps} builder for the specified key and value types.
1158    *
1159    * @param <K> The key type.
1160    * @param <V> The value type.
1161    * @param keyType The key type.
1162    * @param valueType The value type.
1163    * @return A new map builder.
1164    * @see CollectionUtils#mapBuilder(Class,Class)
1165    */
1166   public static <K,V> Maps<K,V> mb(Class<K> keyType, Class<V> valueType) { return CollectionUtils.mapBuilder(keyType, valueType); }
1167
1168   /**
1169    * Creates a new {@link Sets} builder for the specified element type.
1170    *
1171    * @param <E> The element type.
1172    * @param type The element type.
1173    * @return A new set builder.
1174    * @see CollectionUtils#setBuilder(Class)
1175    */
1176   public static <E> Sets<E> stb(Class<E> type) { return CollectionUtils.setBuilder(type); }
1177
1178   // ---- ClassUtils ----
1179
1180   /**
1181    * Casts the specified object to the specified type (null-safe).
1182    *
1183    * @param <T> The target type.
1184    * @param c The target class.
1185    * @param o The object to cast.
1186    * @return The cast object, or null if the object is null.
1187    * @see ClassUtils#castTo(Class,Object)
1188    */
1189   public static <T> T cast(Class<T> c, Object o) { return ClassUtils.castTo(c, o); }
1190
1191   /**
1192    * Makes the specified method accessible.
1193    *
1194    * @param m The method.
1195    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the method was made accessible.
1196    * @see ClassUtils#setAccessible(Method)
1197    */
1198   public static boolean sa(Method m) { return ClassUtils.setAccessible(m); }
1199
1200   /**
1201    * Makes the specified field accessible.
1202    *
1203    * @param f The field.
1204    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the field was made accessible.
1205    * @see ClassUtils#setAccessible(Field)
1206    */
1207   public static boolean sa(Field f) { return ClassUtils.setAccessible(f); }
1208
1209   /**
1210    * Makes the specified constructor accessible.
1211    *
1212    * @param c The constructor.
1213    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the constructor was made accessible.
1214    * @see ClassUtils#setAccessible(Constructor)
1215    */
1216   public static boolean sa(Constructor<?> c) { return ClassUtils.setAccessible(c); }
1217
1218   // ---- ThrowableUtils ----
1219
1220   // Self-contained exception factories (Principle A — no delegation, no log side-effect).
1221
1222   /** Creates a {@link RuntimeException} with a formatted message. */
1223   public static RuntimeException rex(String m, Object...a) { return new RuntimeException(StringUtils.format(m, a)); }
1224
1225   /** Creates a {@link RuntimeException} wrapping a cause. */
1226   public static RuntimeException rex(Throwable t) { return new RuntimeException(t); }
1227
1228   /** Creates a {@link RuntimeException} with a cause and formatted message. */
1229   public static RuntimeException rex(Throwable t, String m, Object...a) { return new RuntimeException(StringUtils.format(m, a), t); }
1230
1231   /** Creates an {@link IllegalArgumentException} with a formatted message. */
1232   public static IllegalArgumentException iaex(String m, Object...a) { return new IllegalArgumentException(StringUtils.format(m, a)); }
1233
1234   /** Creates an {@link IllegalArgumentException} wrapping a cause. */
1235   public static IllegalArgumentException iaex(Throwable t) { return new IllegalArgumentException(t); }
1236
1237   /** Creates an {@link IllegalArgumentException} with a cause and formatted message. */
1238   public static IllegalArgumentException iaex(Throwable t, String m, Object...a) { return new IllegalArgumentException(StringUtils.format(m, a), t); }
1239
1240   /** Creates a {@link BeanRuntimeException} with a formatted message. */
1241   public static BeanRuntimeException brex(String m, Object...a) { return new BeanRuntimeException(m, a); }
1242
1243   /** Creates a {@link BeanRuntimeException} wrapping a cause. */
1244   public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Throwable t) { return new BeanRuntimeException(t); }
1245
1246   /** Creates a {@link BeanRuntimeException} with a cause and formatted message. */
1247   public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Throwable t, String m, Object...a) { return new BeanRuntimeException(StringUtils.format(m, a), t); }
1248
1249   /** Creates a {@link BeanRuntimeException} with an associated class and formatted message. */
1250   public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Class<?> c, String m, Object...a) { return new BeanRuntimeException(c, m, a); }
1251
1252   /** Creates a {@link BeanRuntimeException} with an associated class and formatted message. */
1253   public static BeanRuntimeException brex(ClassInfo c, String m, Object...a) { return new BeanRuntimeException(c.inner(), m, a); }
1254
1255   /** Creates a {@link BeanRuntimeException} with a cause, associated class, and formatted message. */
1256   public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Throwable t, Class<?> c, String m, Object...a) { return new BeanRuntimeException(t, c, m, a); }
1257
1258   /** Creates a {@link BeanRuntimeException} with a cause, associated class, and formatted message. */
1259   public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Throwable t, ClassInfo c, String m, Object...a) { return new BeanRuntimeException(t, c.inner(), m, a); }
1260
1261   /** Creates an {@link IOException} with a formatted message. */
1262   public static IOException ioex(String m, Object...a) { return new IOException(StringUtils.format(m, a)); }
1263
1264   /** Creates an {@link IOException} wrapping a cause. */
1265   public static IOException ioex(Throwable t) { return new IOException(t); }
1266
1267   /** Creates an {@link IOException} with a cause and formatted message. */
1268   public static IOException ioex(Throwable t, String m, Object...a) { return new IOException(StringUtils.format(m, a), t); }
1269
1270   /** Creates an {@link ExecutableException} with a formatted message. */
1271   public static ExecutableException exex(String m, Object...a) { return new ExecutableException(m, a); }
1272
1273   /** Creates an {@link ExecutableException} wrapping a cause. */
1274   public static ExecutableException exex(Throwable t) { return new ExecutableException(t); }
1275
1276   /** Creates an {@link ExecutableException} with a cause and formatted message. */
1277   public static ExecutableException exex(Throwable t, String m, Object...a) { return new ExecutableException(t, m, a); }
1278
1279   /** Creates an {@link IllegalStateException} with a formatted message. */
1280   public static IllegalStateException isex(String m, Object...a) { return new IllegalStateException(StringUtils.format(m, a)); }
1281
1282   /** Creates an {@link UnsupportedOperationException} with the message "Not supported." */
1283   public static UnsupportedOperationException uoex() { return new UnsupportedOperationException("Not supported."); }
1284
1285   /** Creates an {@link UnsupportedOperationException} with a formatted message. */
1286   public static UnsupportedOperationException uoex(String m, Object...a) { return new UnsupportedOperationException(StringUtils.format(m, a)); }
1287
1288   /** Creates an {@link UnsupportedOperationException} wrapping a cause. */
1289   public static UnsupportedOperationException uoex(Throwable t) { return new UnsupportedOperationException(t); }
1290
1291   /** Creates an {@link UnsupportedOperationException} with a cause and formatted message. */
1292   public static UnsupportedOperationException uoex(Throwable t, String m, Object...a) { return new UnsupportedOperationException(StringUtils.format(m, a), t); }
1293
1294   /** Creates an {@link UnsupportedOperationException} with the message "Object is read only." */
1295   public static UnsupportedOperationException uoroex() { return new UnsupportedOperationException("Object is read only."); }
1296
1297   /**
1298    * Returns the stack trace of the specified throwable as a string.
1299    *
1300    * @param t The throwable.
1301    * @return The stack trace string.
1302    * @see ThrowableUtils#getStackTrace(Throwable)
1303    */
1304   public static String gst(Throwable t) { return ThrowableUtils.getStackTrace(t); }
1305
1306   /**
1307    * Casts the specified throwable to the specified type, wrapping if necessary.
1308    *
1309    * @param <T> The target type.
1310    * @param c The target class.
1311    * @param t The throwable.
1312    * @return The cast (or wrapped) throwable.
1313    * @see ThrowableUtils#castException(Class,Throwable)
1314    */
1315   public static <T> T cex(Class<T> c, Throwable t) { return ThrowableUtils.castException(c, t); }
1316
1317   /**
1318    * Searches the causal chain of the specified throwable for a cause of the specified type.
1319    *
1320    * @param <T> The cause type.
1321    * @param e The throwable to search.
1322    * @param c The cause class to look for.
1323    * @return An optional containing the matching cause, if found.
1324    * @see ThrowableUtils#findCause(Throwable,Class)
1325    */
1326   public static <T extends Throwable> Optional<T> fc(Throwable e, Class<T> c) { return ThrowableUtils.findCause(e, c); }
1327
1328   /**
1329    * Returns the first cause of the specified type from the throwable's causal chain.
1330    *
1331    * @param <T> The cause type.
1332    * @param c The cause class to look for.
1333    * @param t The throwable to search.
1334    * @return The matching cause, or null if not found.
1335    * @see ThrowableUtils#getThrowableCause(Class,Throwable)
1336    */
1337   public static <T extends Throwable> T gtc(Class<T> c, Throwable t) { return ThrowableUtils.getThrowableCause(c, t); }
1338
1339   /**
1340    * Returns the localized message of the specified throwable (null-safe).
1341    *
1342    * @param t The throwable.
1343    * @return The localized message, or null if the throwable is null.
1344    * @see ThrowableUtils#localizedMessage(Throwable)
1345    */
1346   public static String lm(Throwable t) { return ThrowableUtils.localizedMessage(t); }
1347
1348   /**
1349    * Runs the specified snippet, silently swallowing any thrown exception.
1350    *
1351    * @param s The snippet to run.
1352    * @see ThrowableUtils#runQuietly(Snippet)
1353    */
1354   public static void quiet(Snippet s) { ThrowableUtils.runQuietly(s); }
1355
1356   // ---- PredicateUtils ----
1357
1358   /**
1359    * Tests the specified value against the given predicate (null-safe).
1360    *
1361    * @param <T> The value type.
1362    * @param p The predicate.
1363    * @param v The value to test.
1364    * @return The result of the predicate, or <jk>false</jk> if the predicate is null.
1365    * @see PredicateUtils#test(Predicate,Object)
1366    */
1367   public static <T> boolean t(Predicate<T> p, T v) { return PredicateUtils.test(p, v); }
1368
1369}
